title: Phase 3: GWAS Mapping
text:
The third phase of the pipeline maps simulated phenotypes using GCTA to evaluate GWA power and precision. It takes the PLINK filesets from Phase 2 and produces per-marker association statistics under four mapping conditions.
The processing flow is:





%%{init: {'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '17px'}}}%%
flowchart TD
    classDef proc fill:#e07b3a,stroke:#a3572a,color:#fff
    classDef mode fill:#fdebd0,stroke:#e07b3a,color:#333
    classDef io fill:#f5f5f5,stroke:#999,color:#333

    PLINK(["Annotated PLINK binaries (Phase 2)"]):::io

    GRM_I["GCTA_MAKE_GRM (inbred)"]:::proc
    GRM_L["GCTA_MAKE_GRM (loco)"]:::proc

    M1["inbred x pca (--qcovar PC1)"]:::mode
    M2["inbred x nopca"]:::mode
    M3["loco x pca (--qcovar PC1)"]:::mode
    M4["loco x nopca"]:::mode

    GWA["GCTA_PERFORM_GWA (4 conditions)"]:::proc

    OUT1([".fastGWA inbred results"]):::io
    OUT2([".mlma loco results"]):::io

    PLINK --> GRM_I & GRM_L
    GRM_I & GRM_L --> GWA
    M1 & M2 & M3 & M4 --> GWA
    GWA --> OUT1 & OUT2






Each process is described below with the exact commands run, key parameters, and links to external tool documentation.
Mode and type parameterization: The mapping phase is parameterized by two variables defined in main.nf:

mode: "inbred" or "loco" — determines the type of GRM and the GWA algorithm. These lowercase values are the canonical algorithm identifiers used in database mapping IDs.
type: "pca" or "nopca" — determines whether the first principal component eigenvector is included as a covariate. type == "pca" maps to pca = TRUE in the database mapping ID.

ch_mode = Channel.of(
    ["inbred", "fastGWA"],
    ["loco", "mlma"]
)

ch_type = Channel.of(
    "pca",
    "nopca"
)
Each simulation replicate is mapped under all four combinations (inbred x pca, inbred x nopca, loco x pca, loco x nopca), driven by Nextflow channel combine operations. The mode crossing happens at GCTA_MAKE_GRM and the type crossing happens at GCTA_PERFORM_GWA.

title: Phase 3: GWAS Mapping
text:
1. GCTA_MAKE_GRM
Source: modules/gcta/make_grm/main.nf
Builds a genetic relatedness matrix (GRM) and estimates phenotypic variance via REML. This process runs two sequential GCTA commands.

Step 1: Build the GRM
The GRM construction method depends on the mode parameter:
if [[ ${mode} == "inbred" ]]; then
    GRM_OPTION="--make-grm-inbred"
else
    GRM_OPTION="--make-grm"
fi

gcta64 --bfile TO_SIMS_${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group} \
        --autosome --maf ${maf} ${GRM_OPTION} \
        --out TO_SIMS_${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_gcta_grm_${mode} \
        --thread-num 1



Flag
Purpose
Docs




--make-grm-inbred
Construct a kinship matrix for inbred organisms, adjusting for reduced heterozygosity
GCTA GRM


--make-grm
Construct a standard genome-wide relatedness matrix
GCTA GRM


--autosome
Use only autosomal markers
GCTA GRM


--maf
Filter markers below the minor allele frequency threshold
GCTA GRM




mode == "inbred": Uses --make-grm-inbred, which constructs a kinship matrix tailored for populations of inbred organisms. The inbred GRM adjusts relatedness estimates to account for the reduced heterozygosity characteristic of inbred lines.
mode == "loco": Uses --make-grm, which constructs a standard genome-wide relatedness matrix using all autosomal markers.

Because main.nf defines ch_mode with both ["inbred", "fastGWA"] and ["loco", "mlma"], each simulation replicate produces two GRMs — one inbred and one standard.


Step 2: Iterative REML variance estimation and scaling
After constructing the GRM, GCTA_MAKE_GRM runs an iterative loop that estimates phenotypic variance (Vp) via REML and scales phenotypes ×1000 per round until Vp >= 1e-4 (max 4 rounds). This prevents near-zero variance from causing numerical failures in GCTA_PERFORM_GWA.
VP_THRESHOLD="0.0001"
VP_MAX_ROUNDS=4
cp -L "${PHEN_INPUT}" working_pheno.txt

for vp_round in $(seq 1 ${VP_MAX_ROUNDS}); do
    gcta64 --grm ${GRM_PREFIX} \
           --pheno working_pheno.txt \
           --reml --out check_vp \
           --thread-num 1

    VP_VALUE=$(awk -F'\t' '$1 == "Vp" {print $2}' check_vp.hsq)

    if [ VP_VALUE >= VP_THRESHOLD ]; then break; fi
    if [ vp_round == VP_MAX_ROUNDS ]; then break; fi

    # Scale phenotype values ×1000
    awk '{printf "%s %s %.10g\n", $1, $2, $3 * 1000}' working_pheno.txt > scaled_pheno.tmp
    mv scaled_pheno.tmp working_pheno.txt
done

cp working_pheno.txt *_sims.pheno
See GCTA GREML analysis documentation.



Flag
Purpose




--grm
Input GRM prefix from step 1


--pheno
Simulated phenotype file (updated each round)


--reml
Perform REML analysis to estimate variance components



If REML fails (e.g., singular information matrix with small/inbred samples), the loop falls back to Vp=1.0 (no scaling).

title: Phase 3: GWAS Mapping
text:
2. GCTA_PERFORM_GWA
Source: modules/gcta/perform_gwa/main.nf
Performs genome-wide association mapping. This process receives the GRM and variance-checked phenotype data, then executes three sub-steps controlled by the mode and type parameters.

Step 1: Create sparse GRM
This step always runs regardless of mode or type:
gcta64 --grm TO_SIMS_${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_gcta_grm_${mode} \
    --make-bK-sparse ${sparse_cut} \
    --out ${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_sparse_grm_${mode} \
    --thread-num 1



Flag
Purpose
Docs




--make-bK-sparse
Set all off-diagonal GRM values with absolute value ≤ sparse_cut (default: 0.05) to zero
GCTA GRM



This produces a sparse representation of the GRM, reducing memory usage for the association mapping step.


Step 2: Conditional PCA
The type parameter controls whether PCA eigenvectors are extracted as covariates:
if [[ ${type} == "pca" ]]; then
    gcta64 --grm TO_SIMS_${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_gcta_grm_${mode} \
        --pca 1 \
        --out ${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_sparse_grm_${mode} \
        --thread-num 1

    COVAR="--qcovar ${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_sparse_grm_${mode}.eigenvec"
else
    COVAR=""
fi

type == "pca": Extracts the first principal component from the GRM (--pca 1), producing an .eigenvec file. This eigenvector is passed as a quantitative covariate (--qcovar) to the mapping command to control for population structure.
type == "nopca": No PCA is performed. The COVAR variable is set to an empty string, so no covariate is included in the mapping command.



Step 3: Association mapping
The mode parameter determines both the GCTA mapping algorithm and the GRM input flag:
if [[ ${mode} == "inbred" ]]; then
    GRM_OPTION='--grm-sparse'
    COMMAND='--fastGWA-mlm-exact'
else
    GRM_OPTION="--grm"
    COMMAND="--mlma-loco"
fi

GWA_THREADS=$( [[ ${mode} == "loco" ]] && echo ${task.cpus} || echo 1 )

gcta64 ${COMMAND} \
    --bfile TO_SIMS_${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group} \
    ${GRM_OPTION} ${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_sparse_grm_${mode} \
    ${COVAR} \
    --out ${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_lmm-exact_${mode}_${type} \
    --pheno ${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_sims.pheno \
    --maf ${maf} \
    --thread-num ${GWA_THREADS}

Thread count: --fastGWA-mlm-exact (inbred mode) uses --thread-num 1 for deterministic results. --mlma-loco (loco mode) uses --thread-num ${task.cpus}, set via the GWA_THREADS shell variable, because its per-chromosome refits are independent and numerically less sensitive. See Thread-count determinism.

Inbred mode (mode == "inbred"):

Uses --fastGWA-mlm-exact to perform an exact mixed linear model (MLM) association analysis (GCTA fastGWA docs)
Uses --grm-sparse to input the sparse GRM created from the inbred relatedness matrix
Output format: .fastGWA (columns: CHR, SNP, POS, A1, A2, N, AF1, BETA, SE, P)

LOCO mode (mode == "loco"):

Uses --mlma-loco to perform MLM-based association using a leave-one-chromosome-out approach, where the GRM excludes the chromosome of the tested variant (GCTA MLMA docs)
Uses --grm to input the sparse GRM created from the standard relatedness matrix
Output format: .loco.mlma (columns: Chr, SNP, bp, A1, A2, Freq, b, se, p), renamed to .mlma after completion

if [[ ${mode} == "loco" ]]; then
    mv "${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_lmm-exact_${mode}_${type}.loco.mlma" \
       "${nqtl}_${rep}_${h2}_${maf}_${effect}_${group}_lmm-exact_${mode}_${type}.mlma"
fi

Mapping identifiers
Each of the four mapping conditions is assigned a unique mapping_id by write_gwa_to_db.R during database migration. The ID is a 20-character SHA-256 hash computed from the parent trait hash and the two condition parameters:
mapping_id = SHA-256("v=1|parent={trait_hash}|algorithm={algorithm}|pca={pca_str}")[0:20]
where:

{trait_hash} is the 20-char parent trait_id hash (itself derived from marker_set_id)
{algorithm} is the lowercased mode value: "inbred" or "loco"
{pca_str} is "TRUE" if type == "pca", otherwise "FALSE"

The full hash hierarchy is:
marker_set_id  (population + maf)
  └── trait_id (marker_set_id + nqtl + effect + rep + h2)
        └── mapping_id (trait_id + algorithm + pca)
Because each level embeds its parent hash, a mapping_id uniquely encodes the entire simulation and mapping parameter chain. generate_mapping_id() normalizes algorithm to lowercase via tolower(). The canonical algorithm values are "inbred" and "loco", matching val(mode) from this process after normalization.
When mapping results are written to the database, only the measurement columns are stored in each data.parquet file:



Column
Description




marker
Marker ID (CHROM:POS format)


AF1
Effect allele frequency


BETA
Effect size estimate


SE
Standard error


P
P-value



mapping_id is the Hive partition key encoded in the directory path (mappings/population={pop}/mapping_id={id}/) and is auto-promoted by DuckDB when the directory tree is scanned — it is not a column inside the Parquet file. marker_set_id and trait_id are in mappings_metadata.parquet (accessible via the metadata DuckDB view). Simulation parameters (nqtl, rep, h2, effect, algorithm, pca, population, maf) are stored once in mappings_metadata.parquet and recovered via joins — they are not repeated in every data row.

All GCTA steps except --mlma-loco are pinned to --thread-num 1 to ensure reproducible floating-point results; see Thread-count determinism in the developer notes for the full rationale.

title: Local End-to-End Testing
text:
Verify pipeline wiring and prepare test inputs before running integration tests. Stub runs check channel topology without executing GCTA or bcftools. Test VCF generation is a one-time setup step required for any non-stub local run.
%%{init: {'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '16px'}}}%%
flowchart LR
    classDef step fill:#4e9af1,stroke:#2c6fad,color:#fff
    classDef check fill:#48b96e,stroke:#2d8150,color:#fff
    classDef data fill:#f5f5f5,stroke:#999,color:#333

    A([Clone repo]):::data
    B[Generate test VCF]:::step
    C[Stub run: -profile test -stub-run]:::step
    D{All processes reached?}:::check
    E[Update container tags]:::step
    F[Stub run again]:::step
    G([Ready for integration testing]):::data

    A --> B --> C --> D
    D -- Yes --> G
    D -- No: wiring issue --> C
    D -- Container update needed --> E --> F --> G

title: Local End-to-End Testing
text:
Pipeline Stub Runs
Stub runs verify process wiring without executing real computations:
# Fixed architecture
NXF_VER=24.10.5 nextflow run main.nf -profile test -stub-run

# Variable architecture
NXF_VER=24.10.5 nextflow run main.nf -profile test_variable -stub-run

# Three-species (c_elegans + c_briggsae + c_tropicalis)
NXF_VER=24.10.5 nextflow run main.nf -profile test_three_species -stub-run

# With legacy assessment path
NXF_VER=24.10.5 nextflow run main.nf -profile test --legacy_assess -stub-run

# CV pool filter — exercises pipeline guard when cv_maf/cv_ld reduce the causal variant pool
NXF_VER=24.10.5 nextflow run main.nf -profile test_cv_pool -stub-run
The test_cv_pool profile sets cv_maf and cv_ld to values that shrink the available causal variant pool relative to the full marker set. The stub run verifies Nextflow channel wiring for the filtered pool code path without executing GCTA.

title: Local End-to-End Testing
text:
Generating Test Data
Test VCF files are not committed to the repository due to their size. Four generate scripts produce subsetted VCFs from the corresponding CaeNDR isotype release. All scripts accept either a local file path or a direct HTTPS URL from the CaeNDR S3 bucket — using a URL is recommended because bcftools fetches only chromosomes I, II, V via tabix random access, avoiding a multi-GB download.
Prerequisites: bcftools >= 1.16, tabix (htslib).

Symlink reference
Each generate script writes a test_{sp}.vcf.gz target and a dated symlink extracted from the source VCF filename. Strainfiles reference the dated names. The table below shows what each profile currently expects (canonical dates are in data/test/release_ids.sh):









Profile(s)
Dated symlink
Target
Generate script




test, test_variable, test_cv_pool
test_ce.20250625.vcf.gz
test_ce.vcf.gz
generate_test_vcf.sh

test_ce.20250625.vcf.gz
test_ce.vcf.gz
generate_test_ce_vcf.sh


test_three_species
test_cb.20250626.vcf.gz
test_cb.vcf.gz
generate_test_cb_vcf.sh


test_three_species
test_ct.20250627.vcf.gz
test_ct.vcf.gz
generate_test_ct_vcf.sh



The dated symlinks are tracked in git; the target files are gitignored. Running a generate script populates the target. tests/collect_test_data.sh performs a pre-flight check before the pipeline starts and exits immediately with an actionable error if any required target is missing.


C. elegans test VCF (test / test_variable profiles)
bash data/test/generate_test_vcf.sh /path/to/WI.YYYYMMDD.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
Produces data/test/test_ce.vcf.gz (chromosomes I/II/V), its tabix index, and a dated symlink test_ce.YYYYMMDD.vcf.gz → test_ce.vcf.gz where the date is extracted from the source VCF filename. The script reads the strains column from the 6-column test_strains.txt strainfile. This VCF is shared by the test, test_variable, and test_cv_pool Nextflow profiles, and uses the same CE release as test_three_species.
See data/test/release_ids.sh for the currently pinned CE release date.

Note (awk fix): This script was updated alongside the S03 strainfile format change — the strains column is now extracted with awk -F'\t' 'NR>1 {print $6}' (column 6, tab-delimited) instead of the old awk '{print $2}'.


On Rockfish: See Generating the Test VCF on Rockfish in the HPC End-to-End Testing page. The key requirement is running export PATH after module load bcftools so that the bash subprocess spawned by the script inherits the updated PATH.



Multi-species test VCFs (test_three_species profile)
The test_three_species profile exercises the full mixed-species code path. It requires VCF files for all three supported species (C. elegans, C. briggsae, C. tropicalis).

Strainfile structure
data/test/test_strains_three_species.txt has six groups — two per species — covering both a small-strain and a large-strain population for each:



Group

The single-population groups ({sp}.test) use all 200 strains. The multi-population groups split those 200 strains evenly into popA (first 100) and popB (last 100). All groups for a species share the same VCF, so only one VCF per species is needed.


Generating the CE, CB, and CT test VCFs
CaeNDR release pages for finding the latest HTTPS URLs:

C. elegans: https://caendr.org/data/data-release/c-elegans/latest
C. briggsae: https://caendr.org/data/data-release/c-briggsae/latest
C. tropicalis: https://caendr.org/data/data-release/c-tropicalis/latest

Run the generate scripts from the project root, passing the S3 URL from each release page:
bash data/test/generate_test_ce_vcf.sh \
  "https://caendr-open-access-data-bucket.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/dataset_release/c_elegans/YYYYMMDD/variation/WI.YYYYMMDD.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz"

bash data/test/generate_test_cb_vcf.sh \
  "https://caendr-open-access-data-bucket.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/dataset_release/c_briggsae/YYYYMMDD/variation/WI.YYYYMMDD.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz"

bash data/test/generate_test_ct_vcf.sh \
  "https://caendr-open-access-data-bucket.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/dataset_release/c_tropicalis/YYYYMMDD/variation/WI.YYYYMMDD.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz"
Each script:

Extracts the 8-digit release date from the source VCF filename
Subsets to chromosomes I, II, V and the first 200 strains by default (pass a custom strain-list file as a second argument to change the selection)
Creates dated symlinks: test_ce.YYYYMMDD.vcf.gz → test_ce.vcf.gz (and .tbi)
Writes the three species rows directly into test_strains_three_species.txt, replacing any existing rows for that species while preserving rows for other species




Running the three-species test
Once VCF files are generated and the strainfile is populated:
NXF_VER=24.10.5 nextflow run . -profile test_three_species,docker
A stub run (no VCF files required) verifies channel wiring and strainfile parsing for all six groups:
nextflow run . -profile test_three_species -stub-run


Working in a git worktree
When developing in a git worktree the VCF targets are absent (gitignored, not copied from the main checkout). If the main repository already has the generated VCFs you can create absolute-path symlinks rather than re-running the generate scripts:
MAIN="/path/to/nemascan-sims-nf/data/test"   # main repo with real VCF files
WT="/path/to/your-worktree/data/test"         # this worktree

# Single-species profiles (test, test_variable, test_cv_pool) — CE VCF only
ln -s "$MAIN/test_ce.vcf.gz"     "$WT/test_ce.vcf.gz"
ln -s "$MAIN/test_ce.vcf.gz.tbi" "$WT/test_ce.vcf.gz.tbi"

# Three-species profile (test_three_species) — run for each species needed
for spec in ce cb ct; do
  ln -s "$MAIN/test_${spec}.vcf.gz"     "$WT/test_${spec}.vcf.gz"
  ln -s "$MAIN/test_${spec}.vcf.gz.tbi" "$WT/test_${spec}.vcf.gz.tbi"
done
Because the targets are gitignored, these symlinks will not appear in git status and cannot be accidentally committed. The existing dated symlinks (e.g. test_ce.20250625.vcf.gz → test_ce.vcf.gz) already tracked in git then resolve through the new absolute-path link to the real file.






TipNext steps



After confirming stub runs pass and test data is in place, proceed to: Local Integration Testing

text:
These tools validate that pipeline output is correct, reproducible, and concordant between the DB path and legacy path. Cross-validation tests compare the two assessment paths. The R package lockfile and GCTA thread-pinning strategy ensure reproducibility across runs and environments.

title: Output Validation
text:
Cross-Validation Tests
Three integration test files are the primary evaluation metric for pipeline correctness. Together they cover database structure, data round-trip fidelity, and assessment concordance between the DB and legacy paths.
test-db_structure.R (27 tests, requires TEST_DB_DIR) — verifies the Parquet database written by DB_MIGRATION_* modules: directory structure, Parquet schemas for all six artifact types (marker sets, genotypes, traits, causal variants, phenotypes, mappings), metadata completeness, DuckDB queryability, and two invariants established by issue111:

Inbred meta$n_markers == marker_set_metadata$n_markers (GCTA processes all markers for fastGWA-mlm-exact)
Loco meta$n_markers < marker_set_metadata$n_markers (GCTA silently excludes near-singular markers from mlma-loco output)

test-assessment_cross_validation.R (10 tests, requires TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT and TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT) — compares db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv (DB path) against simulation_assessment_results.tsv (legacy path). Tests: output column completeness (all 35 format_assessment_tsv() columns), calculate_simrep_performance() runs correctly with Power and FDR in [0, 1], designate_qtl() produces only the 4 valid outcome categories, plus three concordance scores per replicate (detection, designation, interval Jaccard). Requires --legacy_assess when collecting test data.
test-cross_validation.R (8 tests, requires TEST_DB_DIR and TEST_WORK_DIR) — compares DB Parquet contents against raw .fastGWA/.mlma files in the Nextflow work directory. Tests: P/BETA/SE/AF1 round-trip to 1e-12 tolerance, read_raw_gwa_file() normalized column schema for both formats, query_by_mapping_id() coverage for every mapping ID in metadata, EIGEN threshold consistency, and the loco < inbred marker count invariant as a regression guard for issue111.
All three tests skip automatically unless all required env vars are set.

CV Pool Scenario: No Legacy Cross-Validation Path
When testing the test_cv_pool profile (cv_maf=0.01 < ms_maf=0.05), the legacy path is incompatible and test-assessment_cross_validation.R must not be run. Non-marker causal variants (MAF between 0.01 and 0.05) are entirely absent from GWA output files and therefore invisible to Get_GCTA_Intervals.R and Assess_Sims.R. A legacy assessment TSV for this profile would silently misrepresent False Negative rates by omitting these variants from scoring.
The standalone test-cv_pool.R (7 tests, requires TEST_CV_POOL=true) provides DB-path-only quality checks for this scenario:

Confirms that non-marker causal variants appear as Simulated=TRUE, log10p=NA FN rows in the DB assessment output
Validates Simulated.QTL.VarExp is populated for these rows via the per-trait causal genotype path (traits/causal_genotypes/)
Asserts significant=NA (not FALSE) for non-marker rows — distinguishing “absent from GWA” from “tested but below threshold”
Verifies designate_qtl() classifies significant=NA FN rows as Missed.CV so they contribute to the Power denominator in calculate_simrep_performance()
Confirms Power < 1 for at least one replicate (non-marker causal variants are structurally undetectable, reducing achievable power)







NotePower interpretation for cv_pool runs



When cv_maf < ms_maf, the maximum achievable Power is less than 1 because some simulated causal variants are invisible to GWA by design. This is the intended behaviour for studying the effect of rare causal variants below the GWA MAF cutoff — it is not a pipeline bug. The test-cv_pool.R assertion Power < 1 validates this scenario is being exercised.

title: Output Validation
text:
R Package Lockfile
renv.lock at the project root records the exact versions of all R packages installed in the andersenlab/r_packages:20250519 container. This file is a reproducibility audit artifact — it lets you verify which package versions produced a given set of simulation results and reproduce the environment locally if needed.

What is covered
The lockfile covers packages used by R_.* Nextflow processes (bin/ scripts):







Script
Key packages




bin/Get_GenoMatrix_Eigen.R
Rcpp, dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2, stringr, readr, glue, purrr, coop


bin/Create_Causal_QTLs.R
dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2, stringr, readr, IRanges, fuzzyjoin, glue, purrr


bin/Get_GCTA_Intervals.R
dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2, stringr, readr, glue, purrr, ggbeeswarm


bin/Assess_Sims.R
dplyr, tidyr, data.table, GenomicRanges, purrr, glue



DB_MIGRATION_.* processes run in a separate container (andersenlab/migrate-sims:20260202) and are not covered by this lockfile.


Reproducing the environment locally
# Install renv if not present
install.packages("renv")

# Restore the exact package versions recorded in renv.lock
renv::restore(lockfile = "renv.lock")


Regenerating the lockfile
Regenerate renv.lock whenever the r_packages container is rebuilt:
# Local (Docker)
bash scripts/generate_renv_lock.sh docker

# HPC / Rockfish (Singularity)
bash scripts/generate_renv_lock.sh singularity
The script requires an explicit runtime argument (docker or singularity). It pulls andersenlab/r_packages:20250519, runs renv::snapshot(type = "all") inside the container via a bind-mounted project root, and writes renv.lock. Commit the updated file.

title: Output Validation
text:
Thread-Count Determinism
All GCTA steps except --mlma-loco are pinned to --thread-num 1 to ensure reproducible floating-point results across local and HPC runs. Multi-threaded BLAS uses non-deterministic partial-sum reduction order — differences propagate from GRM construction through REML, PCA, and the LMM solve into BETA, SE, and P-values.








GCTA step
--thread-num
Rationale




--make-grm-inbred / --make-grm
1
Errors here propagate to all downstream steps


--reml
1
Governs Vp upscaling decision; must be consistent at borderline values


--simu-qt
1
Ensures phenotype simulation is deterministic for a given random seed


--make-bK-sparse
1
Eliminates residual non-determinism in sparse encoding


--pca 1
1
Eigenvector iteration sensitive to BLAS reduction order


--fastGWA-mlm-exact
1
LMM solve; diverges between local and HPC without pinning


--mlma-loco
configurable (task.cpus)
Per-chromosome refits are independent; highest compute cost; least numerically sensitive step



The gcta_make_grm and gcta_simulate_phenotypes Rockfish labels are configured with cpus = 1 since all their GCTA operations are single-threaded. The gcta_perform_gwa label retains cpus = 4 to provide CPU headroom for --mlma-loco on larger datasets.

BLAS implementation in the production container
gcta64 is a static executable — ldd returns “not a dynamic executable”. Inspect the binary with strings to identify the embedded BLAS:
docker run --rm --platform linux/amd64 \
  quay.io/biocontainers/gcta:1.94.1--h9ee0642_0 \
  sh -c 'strings $(which gcta64) | grep -i -E "mkl|openblas|goto|omp_set_num_threads"'
Result: Intel oneMKL, statically linked, with GNU OpenMP as the thread runtime (gomp_ialias_omp_set_num_threads).

How MKL + OpenMP cause non-determinism in GCTA
Intel MKL parallelises dense linear algebra by splitting work across OpenMP threads and reducing partial results at the end. Floating-point addition is not associative — (a + b) + c ≠ a + (b + c) in IEEE 754 — so the order in which thread-local partial sums are accumulated determines the final value. OpenMP does not guarantee a fixed reduction order, and MKL actively varies its internal tiling and accumulation strategy based on the thread count and the SIMD instruction set available at runtime. The consequences for each GCTA operation are:







Operation
How threading introduces non-determinism




--make-grm[-inbred]
Each GRM entry is a sum of allele products over all markers. MKL threads split the marker axis; partial sums are reduced in thread-completion order, which varies run to run.


--reml
REML is an iterative EM algorithm driven by matrix-vector products on the GRM. Non-deterministic accumulation in each MV product shifts the convergence path, producing different Vp estimates and affecting the GCTA_MAKE_GRM iterative REML scaling decision at borderline values.


--pca 1
Power-iteration eigenvector computation applies repeated MV products. Accumulated rounding differences rotate the eigenvector slightly each run, changing the PC1 covariate value passed to the LMM.


--fastGWA-mlm-exact
The exact LMM solve applies a sparse Cholesky factorization. Thread-parallel factorization produces different fill-in and accumulated roundoff, shifting BETA, SE, and P-values for markers near the significance boundary.


--mlma-loco
Same Cholesky-based LMM, applied once per chromosome. Individual chromosome solves are independent, so parallelism across chromosomes is safe — only within-solve threading (controlled by --thread-num) introduces non-determinism.



MKL also exposes MKL_DYNAMIC, which allows MKL to adjust its own thread count at runtime independent of the application. With MKL_DYNAMIC=TRUE (the MKL default), MKL may use fewer threads than requested if it judges the workload too small — meaning thread count, and therefore reduction order, can differ between runs on the same machine. Pinning --thread-num 1 disables intra-operation parallelism entirely, making reduction order deterministic regardless of MKL_DYNAMIC.


Thread control hierarchy
--thread-num 1 is the primary control and is sufficient for determinism. OMP_NUM_THREADS=1 and MKL_NUM_THREADS=1 are independent env-var overrides that remain effective if any code path bypasses the GCTA flag (e.g., a direct MKL call from a dependency). These are noted as a future belt-and-suspenders hardening option.






TipSee also



For the concordance methodology and score interpretation, see Assessment Cross-Validation.

title: HPC End-to-End Testing
text:
Generating Test VCFs on Rockfish
All test VCF generation scripts must run inside the bcftools Singularity container. --bind /vast/eande106 makes the source VCF paths accessible inside the container; --bind "$PWD" ensures the project directory is writable so generated files land on the host filesystem.
SIMG=/vast/eande106/singularity/quay.io-biocontainers-bcftools-1.16--hfe4b78e_1.img

Single-species profiles (test, test_variable, test_cv_pool)
Only the C. elegans VCF is needed. generate_test_vcf.sh subsets the full CE VCF to 200 strains and chromosomes I, II, V and rewrites data/test/test_strains.txt:
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_test_vcf.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20250625/vcf/WI.20250625.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
Output: data/test/test_ce.vcf.gz (gitignored) and dated symlink test_ce.20250625.vcf.gz. See data/test/release_ids.sh for the currently pinned CE release date.


Three-species profile (test_three_species)
The test_three_species profile requires a separate test VCF for each species. Use the per-species scripts — they apply random variant sampling (15% of variants, for manageable local PLINK marker counts) and update data/test/test_strains_three_species.txt:
# C. elegans
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_test_ce_vcf.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20250625/vcf/WI.20250625.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz

# C. briggsae
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_test_cb_vcf.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_briggsae/WI/variation/20250626/vcf/WI.20250626.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz

# C. tropicalis
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_test_ct_vcf.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_tropicalis/WI/variation/20250627/vcf/WI.20250627.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz
See data/test/release_ids.sh for the currently pinned release dates.

Generating HPC Strainfiles
HPC profiles (test_hpc, test_hpc_variable, test_hpc_cv_pool, test_hpc_three_species) use strainfiles that embed absolute Rockfish VCF paths, so the pipeline reads VCFs directly from the /vast/eande106 filesystem rather than downloading from CaeNDR S3.
data/test/hpc_strains.txt and data/test/hpc_strains_three_species.txt are committed to git as header-only placeholders. Run generate_hpc_strainfile.sh on Rockfish to populate them, using the same Singularity container as above:
SIMG=/vast/eande106/singularity/quay.io-biocontainers-bcftools-1.16--hfe4b78e_1.img

# CE only — updates hpc_strains.txt (used by test_hpc, test_hpc_variable, test_hpc_cv_pool):
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_hpc_strainfile.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20250625/vcf/WI.20250625.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz

# All three species — updates both strainfiles (also needed for test_hpc_three_species):
singularity exec --bind /vast/eande106 --bind "$PWD" $SIMG \
  bash data/test/generate_hpc_strainfile.sh \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_elegans/WI/variation/20250625/vcf/WI.20250625.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz \
  /vast/eande106/data/c_briggsae/WI/variation/20250626/vcf/WI.20250626.hard-filter.isotype.vcf.gz \

text:
Collecting Integration Test Data on Rockfish
Run the pipeline on the cluster for each profile, then use --collect-only on the login node to gather outputs without re-running. Each profile stores outputs in tests/integration_data/<profile>/ so multiple profiles can be collected independently.
Prerequisite: Generate the test VCFs before running any local test profile on Rockfish (see Generating Test VCFs on Rockfish).

Run commands
Each profile uses --output_dir to write to a profile-named directory. This prevents same-day runs from overwriting each other and ensures collect_test_data.sh --collect-only picks up the correct output for each profile.
# Fixed architecture (equivalent to local `test` profile)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test

# Variable architecture grid (equivalent to local `test_variable` profile)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_variable,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_variable \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-variable

# Three-species — requires all three test VCFs (see Generating Test VCFs above)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_three_species,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_three_species \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-three-species

# CV pool — omit --legacy_assess (incompatible with non-marker causal variants)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_cv_pool,rockfish \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_cv_pool \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-cv-pool

--output_dir gives each profile a stable, named output directory. Without it, the default Analysis_Results-{date} collides when multiple profiles are run on the same day.
-work-dir redirects intermediate files to /scratch4/ (high-capacity scratch); without it Nextflow defaults to ./work/ on the login node’s local disk, which is too small for a full pipeline run.
Replace <yourname> with your Rockfish username (e.g. Ryan).
--legacy_assess is required to generate the legacy assessment file used by the cross-validation tests. Omit it for test_cv_pool.



Collecting outputs
After each run completes, collect outputs on the login node:
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test --collect-only
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_variable --collect-only
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_three_species --collect-only
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_cv_pool --collect-only
Sync data to your local machine using rsync from your local terminal (not from Rockfish). <rockfish-host> is whatever SSH alias or hostname you use to connect — check ~/.ssh/config for a Host entry. If you have an alias like Host rockfish, use rockfish; otherwise use the full form <yourname>@login.rockfish.jhu.edu.
# Run from your local machine — syncs db/, assessment TSVs, and cross-validation
# files (work/) for all collected profiles in a single command
rsync -av --delete \
  <rockfish-host>:/vast/eande106/<yourname>/nemascan-sims-nf/tests/integration_data/ \
  tests/integration_data/
The --delete flag keeps the local directory in exact sync with Rockfish (removes any local files that no longer exist on the cluster). The trailing / on both paths syncs the contents of the directory rather than the directory itself.


Running integration tests locally

test profile (fixed architecture, 1 population, 4 mappings)
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test/work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R


test_variable profile (variable architecture, 1 population)
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_variable/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_variable/work \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=256 \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_variable/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_variable/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R


test_three_species profile (3 species, 9 populations, 36 mappings)
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/work \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=36 \
TEST_EXPECTED_MARKER_SETS=9 \
TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS="ce.test.200strains,ce.test.200strains.popA,ce.test.200strains.popB,cb.test.200strains,cb.test.200strains.popA,cb.test.200strains.popB,ct.test.200strains,ct.test.200strains.popA,ct.test.200strains.popB" \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R


test_cv_pool profile (cv pool, no legacy assessment)
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/work \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_CV_POOL=true \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R






NoteEnvironment variables




TEST_WORK_DIR points to a directory containing cross-validation files (.fastGWA, .mlma, *_total_independent_tests.txt). The collect_test_data.sh script extracts only these files from the full Nextflow work directory into a per-profile work/ subdirectory. If unset, test-cross_validation.R skips automatically.
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS defaults to 4. Override for multi-parameter profiles.
TEST_EXPECTED_MARKER_SETS defaults to 1. Override for multi-population profiles.
TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS is required for multi-population assertions (three-species). When unset, multi-population tests skip.

title: HPC End-to-End Testing
text:
Stub Runs on Rockfish
Stub runs bypass SLURM submission and verify process wiring without executing real computations:
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile rockfish \
  --strainfile strains.tsv \
  -stub-run
  # vcf is specified per-group in the strainfile (see README: Strainfile Format)

title: HPC End-to-End Testing
text:
Running HPC Integration Test Profiles
Four HPC profiles mirror the local test profiles but use full CaeNDR VCFs on the Rockfish filesystem. Each combines with rockfish and optionally --legacy_assess. Use --output_dir to give each profile a named output directory — this prevents same-day runs from overwriting each other and ensures collect_test_data.sh --collect-only finds the correct output.
# Fixed architecture (equivalent to local `test` profile)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_hpc \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc

# Variable architecture grid (equivalent to local `test_variable` profile)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc_variable,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_hpc_variable \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-variable

# Three-species (requires hpc_strains_three_species.txt to be populated)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc_three_species,rockfish \
  --legacy_assess \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_hpc_three_species \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-three-species

# CV pool — omit --legacy_assess (incompatible with non-marker causal variants)
nextflow run main.nf \
  -profile test_hpc_cv_pool,rockfish \
  --output_dir Analysis_Results-test_hpc_cv_pool \
  -work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-cv-pool

Collecting HPC outputs
After each run completes, collect outputs on the login node. Pass --work-dir to also collect the cross-validation files (.fastGWA, .mlma, EIGEN files) — only these specific files are extracted from the work directory, not the full contents (which can be 100+ GB).
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc --collect-only \
  --work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc

bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc_variable --collect-only \
  --work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-variable

bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc_three_species --collect-only \
  --work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-three-species

bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_hpc_cv_pool --collect-only \
  --work-dir /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-cv-pool
The collect script looks for Analysis_Results-<profile>/ first, then falls back to the most recent Analysis_Results-* directory. Cross-validation files are stored in tests/integration_data/<profile>/work/ alongside db/ and assessment TSVs.


Syncing to local machine
Run from your local terminal (not Rockfish). A single rsync syncs everything — db/, assessment TSVs, and cross-validation work files — for all collected profiles:
rsync -av --delete \
  <rockfish-host>:/vast/eande106/<yourname>/nemascan-sims-nf/tests/integration_data/ \
  tests/integration_data/


Running HPC integration tests locally
HPC profiles use population name ce.hpc (not ce.test.200strains), so TEST_POPULATION must be set for all HPC test invocations.

test_hpc profile (fixed architecture, 1 population, 4 mappings)
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc/work \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.hpc \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R


test_hpc_variable profile (variable architecture, 1 population, 260 mappings)
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_variable/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_variable/work \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.hpc \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=260 \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_variable/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_variable/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R


test_hpc_three_species profile (9 populations, 36 mappings)
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_three_species/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_three_species/work \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.hpc \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=36 \
TEST_EXPECTED_MARKER_SETS=9 \
TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS="ce.hpc,ce.hpc.popA,ce.hpc.popB,cb.hpc,cb.hpc.popA,cb.hpc.popB,ct.hpc,ct.hpc.popA,ct.hpc.popB" \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_three_species/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
If the three-species profile was run with --legacy_assess, also add:
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_three_species/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \


test_hpc_cv_pool profile (cv pool, no legacy assessment)
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_cv_pool/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_cv_pool/work \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.hpc \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_hpc_cv_pool/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_CV_POOL=true \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R






TipAfter collecting HPC data



Run the full integration test suite locally: Local Integration Testing

title: HPC End-to-End Testing
text:
Cleaning Up Work Directories
After collecting integration test data and syncing to your local machine, remove the Nextflow work directories from scratch to free space on /scratch4/.
Run these commands on Rockfish (not your local machine). Replace <yourname> with your Rockfish username.

Local test profiles
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-variable
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-three-species
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-cv-pool


HPC profiles
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-variable
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-three-species
rm -rf /scratch4/eande106/<yourname>/nf-work-test-hpc-cv-pool






WarningVerify data is synced before deleting



Confirm your local tests/integration_data/ is up to date and integration tests pass before removing work directories. The cross-validation test (test-cross_validation.R) reads raw GWA files from the work directory — once deleted, that test will skip automatically.

title: Local Integration Testing
text:
The test suite is organized around integration tests as the primary evaluation metric. Integration tests run against real pipeline output and catch bugs that static fixtures cannot — GCTA marker exclusions, Hive partition path construction, DuckDB view registration, Nextflow channel wiring, and schema drift between what R writes and what downstream queries read. Unit tests support them by pinning pure-computation correctness (hash determinism, ANOVA golden values, threshold math) where no real pipeline output is needed or helpful.
%%{init: {'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '16px'}}}%%
flowchart TD
    classDef primary fill:#48b96e,stroke:#2d8150,color:#fff
    classDef supporting fill:#7ab0d4,stroke:#4a80a4,color:#fff
    classDef gate fill:#e07b3a,stroke:#a3572a,color:#fff
    classDef data fill:#f5f5f5,stroke:#999,color:#333

    A([Rscript tests/run_tests.R]):::data
    B{TEST_DB_DIR set?}:::gate
    C[Unit tests — spec and regression guards]:::supporting
    D[Integration tests — primary evaluation]:::primary
    E[pytest tests/pytest/]:::supporting
    F([Results reported]):::data

    A --> B
    B -- No --> C --> F
    B -- Yes --> C --> F
    B -- Yes --> D --> F
    A --> E --> F

Test Tiers

Run all tiers with a single command; integration tests skip automatically when their environment variables are absent:
Rscript tests/run_tests.R






Notetest-verify_db.R (8 tests)



The unit tier contains one test file dedicated to bin/verify_db.R, the post-run database completeness checker. It builds a tempdir fixture DB from the real database.R hash helpers and exercises verify_db() in both expectation and discovery modes, plus all four plot helpers. It runs without any environment variables and without a pipeline invocation. See Verifying Simulation Outputs for the script itself.

title: Local Integration Testing
text:
Integration Tests
Integration tests are the primary evaluation metric for pipeline correctness. They run against real pipeline output produced by tests/collect_test_data.sh and are the authoritative check that the pipeline is working correctly end-to-end. Unit tests check isolated R functions with static fixtures; integration tests catch the broader class of bugs that only appear with real GCTA output — edge cases in file format parsing, Hive partition path construction, DuckDB view registration, Nextflow channel wiring, and schema drift between what R writes and what queries read.

test-db_structure.R (27 tests)
Verifies the Parquet database written by DB_MIGRATION_* modules. Requires TEST_DB_DIR.







Test group
What it checks




Directory structure
markers/, mappings/, traits/, traits/causal_variants/, traits/phenotypes/ all exist


Marker set files
Parquet files exist; filenames derived from metadata hash lookup


Hive partitions
population= / mapping_id= directories exist with data.parquet files


Metadata files
mappings_metadata.parquet present; marker_set_metadata/ directory with per-population *_metadata.parquet files


Marker set metadata
Required columns; strainfile_hash is 64-char SHA-256; strain_list non-empty


Marker set schema
Correct 6-column schema; FK columns absent from data files


Mappings metadata
Core columns present; algorithm values "inbred"/"loco"; both PCA flags present; mapping IDs are 20-char hex


Genotype matrix schema
Long-format schema: marker_set_id utf8, CHROM utf8, POS int32, strain utf8, allele float64


Trait metadata schema
12 columns including nqtl int32, h2 float64; trait_id utf8


Phenotype schema
strain/phenotype columns; no NA phenotype values


Causal variants schema
6 columns; QTL values in CHROM:POS format


Inbred marker invariant
meta$n_markers == marker_set_metadata$n_markers — GCTA processes all markers for inbred


Loco marker invariant
meta$n_markers == marker_set_metadata$n_markers — after the LOCO marker count fix, loco stores the full marker set size in metadata (same as inbred)


Database queryability
open_mapping_db(), DuckDB table listing, COUNT(*) query, db_stats()


Mapping data integrity
All partitions non-empty; P ∈ (0, 1]; no NULL P; var.exp absent or all-NA


Query functions
query_mapping_data() returns joined data with CHROM/POS; get_threshold_params() returns valid BF and EIGEN thresholds





test-cross_validation.R (8 tests)
Compares DB Parquet contents against raw GWA files in the Nextflow work directory. Requires TEST_DB_DIR and TEST_WORK_DIR.







Test
What it checks




P and BETA round-trip
DB values match raw .fastGWA/.mlma files to 1e-12 tolerance


SE round-trip
DB SE values match raw source


AF1 round-trip
DB AF1 values match raw source


EIGEN threshold
DB n_independent_tests matches the pipeline EIGEN file


GWA column normalization
read_raw_gwa_file() returns 9 normalized columns (marker, CHROM, POS, A1, A2, AF1, BETA, SE, P) for both .fastGWA and .mlma formats


Full coverage
query_by_mapping_id() returns a non-empty result for every mapping_id in metadata


Marker count consistency
meta$n_markers matches actual partition row counts


Loco/inbred invariant
loco$n_markers == inbred$n_markers for the same marker set — both modes store equal marker counts after the LOCO marker count fix





test-assessment_cross_validation.R (12 tests)
Compares db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv (DB path) against simulation_assessment_results.tsv (legacy path). Requires TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT and TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT.







Test
What it checks




File existence
Both assessment TSVs present and non-empty


Hash determinism
generate_mapping_id() produces identical 20-char hash across two independent calls


Column completeness
DB assessment has exactly 35 columns matching the format_assessment_tsv() schema


calculate_simrep_performance()
Runs without error on real data; Power and FDR are in [0, 1]


designate_qtl() categories
Only the 4 valid designations (Detected.CV, Missed.CV, CV.Not.Significant.In.Interval, False.Discovery) appear in real data


Detection concordance
Detected flag agreement between DB and legacy paths is 1.0 for all mappings


Designation concordance
designate_qtl() category agreement is 1.0 for all mappings


Interval concordance
Jaccard overlap of [startPOS, endPOS] intervals is 1.0 for all detected QTLs


VarExp populated
DB assessment has at least some non-NA Simulated.QTL.VarExp values


VarExp concordance
Simulated.QTL.VarExp matches between DB and legacy paths within 1e-6


Per-population concordance
Detection concordance holds independently within each strain_set_id (multi-population guard)


Chromosome name consistency
CHROM portion of QTL column matches between DB and legacy paths (multi-species guard)





test-cv_pool.R (7 tests)
Validates the test_cv_pool scenario where cv_maf < ms_maf, producing causal variants absent from GWA output. Requires TEST_DB_DIR, TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT, and TEST_CV_POOL=true. Do not set TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT for this scenario — the legacy path is incompatible (non-marker causal variants are invisible to it).







Test
What it checks




Causal genotypes directory
traits/causal_genotypes/ exists with non-empty parquet files


Causal genotype schema
(trait_id, QTL, CHROM, POS, strain, allele) columns present


Non-marker FN rows
Assessment output contains rows with Simulated=TRUE, log10p=NA


significant=NA pattern
Non-marker FN rows have significant=NA (not FALSE) — distinguishes “absent from GWA”


VarExp for non-marker rows
Simulated.QTL.VarExp is populated for non-marker FN rows (per-trait causal genotype path)


Non-marker in causal but not markers
Causal QTLs absent from DuckDB markers view — confirms superset pool was exercised


designate_qtl() classification
Non-marker FN rows (significant=NA) classified as Missed.CV for Power denominator


Standalone Power/FDR
calculate_simrep_performance() runs correctly; Power < 1 for at least one replicate





Environment Variables








Variable
Purpose
Required By




TEST_DB_DIR
Path to pipeline Parquet database ({outputDir}/db/)
All integration tests


TEST_WORK_DIR
Path to Nextflow work directory (.nf-work/)
test-cross_validation.R


TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT
Path to simulation_assessment_results.tsv (legacy path)
test-assessment_cross_validation.R


TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT
Path to db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv (DB path)
test-assessment_cross_validation.R, test-cv_pool.R


TEST_POPULATION
Expected population name for single-population assertions
test-db_structure.R (default: ce.test.200strains)


TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS
Expected nrow(mappings_metadata)
test-db_structure.R (default: 4)


TEST_EXPECTED_MARKER_SETS
Expected number of per-population metadata files
test-db_structure.R (default: 1)


TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS
Comma-separated list of all expected population names
test-db_structure.R (enables multi-population assertions)


TEST_CV_POOL
Set to true to activate cv_pool-specific assertions
test-cv_pool.R





Collecting Integration Test Data
Integration tests require pipeline output collected by tests/collect_test_data.sh. Each profile stores artifacts in its own subdirectory so outputs can be tested independently:
# --- Default test profile (fixed architecture, 1 population) ---
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh
# Artifacts: tests/integration_data/test/

# Run integration tests
TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
# --- test_variable profile (8 nQTL × 4 h² × 2 reps = 256 mappings) ---
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_variable
# Artifacts: tests/integration_data/test_variable/

TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_variable/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_variable/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_variable/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=256 \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
# --- test_three_species profile (3 species × 3 populations = 9 groups, 36 mappings) ---
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_three_species
# Artifacts: tests/integration_data/test_three_species/

TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work \
TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_three_species/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS=36 \
TEST_EXPECTED_MARKER_SETS=9 \
TEST_POPULATION=ce.test \
TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS=ce.test,ce.test.popA,ce.test.popB,cb.test,cb.test.popA,cb.test.popB,ct.test,ct.test.popA,ct.test.popB \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
# --- test_cv_pool profile (cv_maf=0.01 < ms_maf=0.05; no legacy assess) ---
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_cv_pool --no-legacy
# Artifacts: tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/
# Note: do NOT set TEST_LEGACY_ASSESSMENT for this profile

TEST_DB_DIR=tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/db \
TEST_WORK_DIR=tests/.nf-work \
TEST_DB_ASSESSMENT=tests/integration_data/test_cv_pool/db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv \
TEST_CV_POOL=true \
Rscript tests/run_tests.R
The --collect-only and --clean flags work with all profiles:
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_variable --collect-only  # collect without re-running
bash tests/collect_test_data.sh --profile test_variable --clean         # clean then run
Docker must be available for all local integration test collection runs. Integration test files skip automatically when their required environment variables are not set.






TipRelated pages




To collect integration test data on the HPC: HPC End-to-End Testing
For cross-validation and output validation tests: Output Validation

title: Changelog
text: All notable changes to nemascan-sims-nf are recorded here. The project does not use version tags; entries are organized by development phase and date.

title: Changelog
text:
[Unreleased] — May 2026

Changed

In-pipeline QTL detection + assessment fanout is now opt-in via --run_postprocess (fix/perform-gwa-concurrent-mod-exception, 2026-05-18): The DB_MIGRATION_ANALYZE_QTL + DB_MIGRATION_ASSESS_SIMS fanout and the db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv collectFile are now wrapped in if (params.run_postprocess) { … }. The default is false, which is a behavior change: out-of-repo callers (CaeNDR production, user scripts) that do not pass the flag will no longer see the assessment TSV at the end of a run. The Parquet DB itself is unchanged — it is always written, regardless of the flag — so offline analysis against the populated DB remains possible. A log.info reminder is emitted at pipeline start whenever run_postprocess = false to mitigate silent surprise. DB_MIGRATION_AGGREGATE_METADATA is intentionally not gated: it feeds ch_db_analysis_barrier and the VALIDATE_REPLICATION_COMPLETE trigger, both of which must remain unconditional. ch_db_assessment_pub is initialized to Channel.empty() outside the gate so the publish stanza at the bottom of main.nf continues to compile when the gate is closed — publishing an empty channel is a no-op. The flag was introduced alongside the ConcurrentModificationException fix in GCTA_PERFORM_GWA so that the downstream analyze/assess processes (susceptible to similar channel-recombination races) can be held offline until they receive an equivalent fix.
Halt-with-replay-manifest error handling (fix/error-handling, 2026-05-12): Post-fanout processes now drop on retry exhaustion via errorStrategy = 'ignore' instead of terminating the pipeline. Failed slots are recorded to ${output_dir}/.failures/<key>.json by a bash EXIT trap (bin/failure_trap.sh), aggregated to ${output_dir}/replay.tsv in workflow.onComplete, and recovered by a second invocation: -profile replay --replay <tsv> -resume. DB_CLEAN_REPLAY_SLOTS removes the 12 partial-write files per replayed slot (4 trait + 8 mapping) before the replay rewrites them. VALIDATE_REPLICATION_COMPLETE warns on short replication but does not fail the run — the pipeline exits 0 even when reps were dropped. Prep-phase processes (bcftools_*, plink_recode_*, r_find_genotype_matrix_eigen) stay on terminate (a prep-phase failure is cone-level, not rep-level). See Replay & Error Handling for the operator runbook, operational constraints (output_dir bind-mount, manual replay.tsv rotation, scratch retention window), the statistical caveat for replayed reps, and known limitations (replay.tsv archival, SIGKILL safety net, and REPLAY_REQUIRED flag file are not yet implemented).

title: Changelog
text:
[Unreleased] — April 2026

Removed

Replicate-based RNG seeding removed from causal variant scripts (refactor/rep-seed-strat, 2026-04-23): The fix/rand-seed-stochastic-scripts fix used rep directly as the RNG seed, which prevented scaling up simulations (adding replicates reused existing seeds) and created seed collisions when failed replicates were re-run with a new ID. Because GCTA phenotype simulation is itself non-deterministic (no --seed flag), there is no pipeline-wide reproducibility to preserve.

bin/create_causal_vars.py: np.random.default_rng(rep) → np.random.default_rng() (OS-entropy seed)
bin/Create_Causal_QTLs.R: set.seed(as.integer(rep)) call removed
trait_metadata_schema() sim_seed int32 field removed; write_trait_metadata() signature updated
write_trait_data.R no longer passes sim_seed
test-db_structure.R updated to expect 12-column trait metadata schema (no sim_seed)

title: Changelog
text:
[Unreleased] — March 2026

Fixed

DB_MIGRATION_ANALYZE_QTL / DB_MIGRATION_ASSESS_SIMS “Too many open files” (fix/db-qtl-io-error, 2026-04-09): A production Rockfish run (Sims_ce-cb-ct_nqtl1-50_h2grid_50reps) hit 2,024 task failures with IO Error: Cannot open file ... Too many open files in system. Root cause: every analyze_qtl / assess_sims task called query_for_threshold_analysis() which routes through open_mapping_db() in R/queries.R. That function executes CREATE VIEW mappings AS SELECT * FROM read_parquet('.../db/mappings/**/data.parquet', union_by_name = true), and DuckDB must open every parquet file at query-prepare time to compute the unified schema — 16,608 files on the failing run, replicated across ~100 concurrent tasks (cross-contamination was the smoking gun: tasks crashed trying to open unrelated partitions). Each task already knows its own population and mapping_id at launch time, so the global view is unnecessary. The fix adds query_mapping_direct(mapping_id,   population, ms_meta, base_dir) in R/queries.R that reads db/mappings/population={pop}/mapping_id={id}/data.parquet directly via arrow::read_parquet(), reuses read_marker_set() for the CHROM/POS lookup, and preserves the LOCO CHROM:POS uniqueness check verbatim. The two hot-path call sites (analyze_qtl.R:108, assess_sims.R:203) now use the direct helper; per-task file opens drop from ≈16,608 to ≤3. open_mapping_db() and query_for_threshold_analysis() stay intact for bulk/aggregate queries. No DB regeneration, no schema change, no Nextflow changes. A new schema-parity test in test-cross_validation.R asserts the direct and legacy paths return equivalent dataframes. Full RCA: issues/new-db-qtl-analysis-too-many-files/rca.md.



Added

Multi-profile integration testing support (fix/merge-unit-with-integration, 2026-03-23): Extended the integration test framework to cover four testing profiles beyond the default test baseline.

tests/collect_test_data.sh now accepts --profile <name>, --no-legacy, and --clean flags; artifacts are stored in tests/integration_data/<profile>/ subdirectories so each profile’s outputs can be tested independently
test-db_structure.R gains TEST_EXPECTED_MARKER_SETS and TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS env var guards; the inbred/loco marker count invariants now loop over all populations (not just [1,]) to cover multi-population and multi-species runs
test-cross_validation.R: per-population LOCO < INBRED marker count loop
test-assessment_cross_validation.R gains per-population concordance breakdown and chromosome-naming consistency check (multi-species guard)
test-cv_pool.R (new, 7 tests): cv_pool-specific assertions — causal genotypes directory and schema, non-marker FN row presence and significant=NA pattern, Simulated.QTL.VarExp for non-marker positions via per-trait causal genotypes, non-marker causal QTLs absent from markers DB view, standalone Power/FDR check

HPC integration testing documentation (fix/merge-unit-with-integration, 2026-03-27): docs/testing-hpc.qmd now provides exact per-profile test invocation commands for all 8 profiles (4 local + 4 HPC) with the correct environment variables (TEST_POPULATION, TEST_EXPECTED_MAPPINGS, TEST_EXPECTED_MARKER_SETS, TEST_EXPECTED_POPULATIONS). All pipeline run commands use --output_dir   Analysis_Results-<profile> to prevent same-day output collisions. Per-profile work directory rsync commands added for cross-validation testing.



Fixed

Concurrent write race in marker set metadata on HPC (fix/merge-unit-with-integration, 2026-03-26): write_marker_set_metadata() previously appended to a single shared marker_set_metadata.parquet file using a read-modify-write pattern. When multiple DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_MARKER_SET SLURM jobs ran in parallel (e.g., 9 jobs for the three-species HPC test profile), concurrent writes caused lost updates — population records were silently overwritten. Downstream processes (WRITE_GWA_TO_DB, WRITE_TRAIT_DATA) then failed with “Marker set metadata not found”. The fix replaces the single shared file with per-population metadata files (marker_set_metadata/{population}_{maf}_metadata.parquet), eliminating the shared mutable resource entirely.
designate_qtl() correctly classifies all non-marker causal variants (fix/merge-unit-with-integration, 2026-03-27): when cv_maf < ms_maf, causal variants absent from GWA output receive significant=NA. Previously case_when produced designation=NA for these rows. The fix handles both cases:

Detected=TRUE, significant=NA (non-marker variant inside a detected QTL interval) → Detected.CV — correctly localized despite no direct GWA test
Detected=FALSE, significant=NA (non-marker variant not in any interval) → Missed.CV — contributes to the Power denominator as a false negative

collect_test_data.sh prevents nested db/db/ on re-collection (fix/merge-unit-with-integration, 2026-03-27): cp -R into an existing destination created a nested copy. The script now removes the stale db/ directory before copying. Additionally, the script now uses --output_dir   Analysis_Results-<profile> when running the pipeline and looks for the profile-named output directory first when collecting, preventing cross-profile contamination when multiple profiles are run on the same day.
Selective cross-validation file collection from work directory (fix/merge-unit-with-integration, 2026-03-27): collect_test_data.sh gains --work-dir <path> flag that extracts only the files needed by test-cross_validation.R (.fastGWA, .mlma, *_total_independent_tests.txt) into a per-profile work/ subdirectory. This replaces syncing the full Nextflow work directory (100+ GB on HPC) — the collected cross-validation files are typically < 5% of that. Each profile now has its own work/ directory alongside db/ and assessment TSVs, eliminating the shared tests/.nf-work/ bottleneck.



Changed

Integration tests are now the sole evaluation framework (fix/merge-unit-with-integration, 2026-03-23): Static-fixture unit tests (test-*.R in tests/testthat/ except the three integration files) removed. The integration framework was extended to cover the behaviors they tested — now evaluated against real pipeline output rather than synthetic fixtures.

test-db_structure.R gains 7 new tests: traits/ subdirectory existence, genotype matrix Arrow schema, trait metadata schema (sim_seed int32), phenotype schema (no-NA check), causal variants schema (QTL in CHROM:POS format), inbred n_markers == marker_set_metadata$n_markers, and loco n_markers < marker_set_metadata$n_markers (issue111 regression guard; 27 tests total)
test-assessment_cross_validation.R gains 3 new tests: DB assessment column completeness (35 columns from format_assessment_tsv()), calculate_simrep_performance() on real data, designate_qtl() valid-category assertion (10 tests total)
test-cross_validation.R gains 3 new tests: read_raw_gwa_file() normalized column schema for both .fastGWA and .mlma, query_by_mapping_id() full coverage, loco < inbred marker count invariant (8 tests total)




Fixed

Eigen calculation uses full decomposition (Li & Ji 2005) (fix-eigen-calculation, 2026-03-20): Replaced the RSpectra::eigs_sym() partial-decomposition while-loop in bin/Get_GenoMatrix_Eigen.R with a full eigen() decomposition implementing the Li & Ji

method. The old code clipped eigenvalues >1 to exactly 1 and stopped accumulating when the incremental sum stopped growing — missing the fractional contributions of small eigenvalues and causing n_independent_tests to be ~2 orders of magnitude too low. The corrected formula sums 1 for each λ > 1 and the fractional part of each λ ≤ 1, producing a stricter EIGEN significance threshold. The RSpectra package dependency is removed. Integration test data regenerated to reflect the corrected threshold.




Fixed

Missing versions.yml in DB migration modules (fix/versions_yml_db_migration, 2026-03-05): Added versions.yml output to all 7 modules/db_migration/*/main.nf processes (write_marker_set, write_genotype_matrix, write_gwa_to_db, write_trait_data, aggregate_metadata, analyze_qtl, assess_sims). The pattern was established across all other module families (bcftools, GCTA, PLINK, R, Python) but had not been carried forward when the DB migration layer was added. All 7 processes now capture the R version and wire their versions output channel into ch_versions in main.nf.



Fixed

Pipeline configuration gaps (fix/pipeline_configuration_gaps, 2026-03-05):

Added --output_dir parameter for stable, deterministic output directory naming. The date-based default (Analysis_Results-{date}) is preserved when --output_dir is not specified.
Added nextflow_schema.json — machine-readable parameter schema covering all pipeline parameters, compatible with nf-core tooling and IDE parameter validation.
Documented previously undocumented parameters --alpha and --legacy_assess in the README parameters table and --help output.
Parameterized hardcoded Rockfish cluster paths in conf/rockfish.config: --slurm_account (replaces hardcoded eande106 in clusterOptions) and --scratch_dir (replaces hardcoded /scratch4/eande106 in workflow.workDir). All Rockfish infrastructure params are now overridable without editing the config file.




Fixed

Stochastic script RNG wired through single Generator (fix/rand-seed-stochastic-scripts, 2026-03-05): causal variant selection and effect size simulation calls (rng.choice, rng.gamma, rng.uniform) were unified under a single NumPy Generator instance in create_causal_vars.py. Previously, simulate_effect_gamma() called np.random.default_rng() internally, creating an independent unseeded Generator. The rep-based seeding added in this fix was subsequently removed — see refactor/rep-seed-strat above.



Added

Non-marker causal variants (feature/add-nonmarker-cvs, 2026-03-03): causal variants can now be drawn from a separately-configured variant pool rather than the marker SNP set. Key changes:

Strainfile format extended to 6 columns: group, species, vcf, ms_maf, ms_ld, strains. Species, VCF source, and marker-set thresholds are now per-group in the strainfile; --vcf, --maf, --species, --vcf_release_id, --ms_maf, and --ms_ld CLI parameters removed.
New CLI parameters --cv_maf (default: each group’s ms_maf) and --cv_ld (default: 0.8) configure the causal variant pool MAF and LD thresholds independently of the marker SNP set.
Two parallel PLINK runs per group: PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF (marker SNP set, drives GWA) and PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF (CV pool, drives causal variant selection). Mixed-species runs supported in a single pipeline invocation.
PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL now selects causal variants from CV_TO_SIMS.bim (CV pool BIM); GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES now uses --bfile CV_TO_SIMS.
Marker set ID upgraded to v=2: SHA-256 now encodes population, maf, species, vcf_release_id, and ms_ld. CV parameters (cv_maf, cv_ld) are intentionally excluded from the hash.
Marker set metadata gains three new columns: species (utf8), vcf_release_id (utf8), ms_ld (float64).
DB write scripts (write_marker_set.R, write_genotype_matrix.R, write_gwa_to_db.R) and their Nextflow modules updated to accept and pass --species, --vcf_release_id, --ms_ld.
aggregate_metadata.R fallback path updated to emit NA for the three new columns when no metadata sidecars exist.




Fixed

Python dependency pinning (fix/python-requirements, 2026-03-05): added requirements.txt at the repository root pinning numpy==2.2.3 and pandas==2.2.3 to the versions present in the andersenlab/numpy:20250506 container. The PYTHON_SIMULATE_EFFECTS_GLOBAL versions.yml emission now also records numpy and pandas library versions alongside the Python interpreter version, providing runtime provenance in pipeline output logs.



Added

R package lockfile (fix/r_package_lockfile, 2026-03-05): renv.lock committed at the project root records the exact versions of all R packages installed in the andersenlab/r_packages:20250519 container. Provides a verifiable record of the software environment that produced simulation results and enables local environment reproduction via renv::restore(). scripts/generate_renv_lock.sh documents how to regenerate the lockfile when the container changes.



Added

Strainfile provenance in marker set metadata (fix/strain-file-not-hashed, 2026-03-05): Marker set metadata now records two new columns for reproducibility auditing:

strainfile_hash (utf8) — SHA-256 hex digest of the complete strainfile, computed once at pipeline submission time (same value for all population groups in a run).
strain_list (utf8) — Comma-separated list of strain names used for the specific population group, derived from the strainfile at submission time.
Both columns are optional/NA-safe: queries against databases produced before this change return NULL without error.
Enables post-hoc verification that a database was produced with a particular strain set, and that the strainfile has not been modified between runs.




Changed

Mapping data Parquet schema: FK columns removed (reproducibility-updates, 2026-03-10): mappings/population={pop}/mapping_id={id}/data.parquet now contains only the five measurement columns (marker, AF1, BETA, SE, P). The three foreign-key columns previously stored in every data file have been relocated:

mapping_id — already encoded in the Hive directory path; DuckDB auto-promotes it as a virtual column when the directory tree is scanned, so no query changes are needed.
marker_set_id and trait_id — moved to mappings_metadata.parquet, accessible via the metadata DuckDB view. This removes constant-per-mapping columns from the millions-of-rows data files without losing any information.




Added

mappings_metadata.parquet sidecar file written to the database root by DB_MIGRATION_AGGREGATE_METADATA — provides a single queryable index of all mapping runs without scanning every Hive partition. (2026-03-02)

title: Changelog
text:
Phase 5 — Database Integration

PR #98 — Hash-based IDs (2026-03-01)
Replaced sequential or filename-derived identifiers with deterministic 12-character MD5 hex IDs, making all IDs stable across -resume runs.

Added

generate_trait_id(group, maf, nqtl, effect, rep, h2) — MD5 hash of concatenated parameters; used as the primary key for all per-trait Parquet files (traits/, causal_variants/, phenotypes/).
generate_mapping_id(trait_id, mode, type) — MD5 hash used as the Hive partition key under mappings/population={pop}/mapping_id={id}/.
generate_marker_set_id(population, maf, species, vcf_release_id, ms_ld) — SHA-256 hash (v=2) written to per-population marker set metadata and used in the marker-set file names.
Unit tests for all three hash functions in test-hash_generation.R, with golden-value assertions to catch silent hash drift.



Changed

Mappings Parquet schema reduced to core columns; accessible metadata (population, MAF, effect, nQTL, h2, replicate) is no longer duplicated in the mappings table — it is retrievable via the trait ID foreign key.
Allele frequency (AF) column removed from the marker-set schema.
Algorithm names standardized to loco / inbred across all pipeline paths (both DB and legacy) to replace inconsistent earlier labels.
Trait storage tests updated to cover hash-based ID determinism and round-trip write/read.



Fixed

Query functions updated to remove the defunct AF join that referenced a column no longer in the schema.
Trait id generation corrected to use the hash framework (previously still used a non-hash method after the schema change).




PR #97 — Automated Cross-Validation Tests (2026-02-27)

Added

test-assessment_cross_validation.R — compares db_simulation_assessment_results.tsv (DB path) against simulation_assessment_results.tsv (legacy path), computing per-replicate detection concordance, designation concordance, and interval overlap (Jaccard score).
test-cross_validation.R — round-trip check comparing Parquet DB contents against raw .fastGWA/.mlma files in the Nextflow work directory.




PR #96 — Phase 5: Genotype & Trait Storage (2026-02-21)
Extended the Parquet database to capture the full data needed for offline variance-explained estimation and richer downstream queries.

Added

Genotype matrix storage — write_genotype_matrix() / read_genotype_matrix() in R/database.R; long-format Parquet files ({pop}_{maf}_genotypes.parquet) written to marker_sets/.
DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX Nextflow module (modules/db_migration/write_genotype_matrix/).
Trait data storage — three write functions (write_trait_metadata(), write_causal_variants(), write_phenotype_data()) and corresponding Parquet schemas in R/database.R.
DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_TRAIT_DATA Nextflow module (modules/db_migration/write_trait_data/); uses .merge() to align GCTA_SIMULATE_PHENOTYPES output channels by emission order.
Phenotype stored in phenotypes/ is the pre-upscaling GCTA .phen output (before GCTA_MAKE_GRM iterative REML scaling), making the ANOVA SS ratio for offline variance-explained scale-invariant.
Unit tests in test-genotype_storage.R and test-trait_storage.R.
Test fixture: tests/fixtures/test_genotype_matrix.tsv (5 markers × 3 strains) and tests/fixtures/test_sims.phen (3-strain GCTA .phen file).



Changed

var.exp column removed from mappings_schema() and prepare_mapping_data(); it was always NA in the DB path. Raw data for offline estimation is now available in traits/, causal_variants/, and phenotypes/.
All DB write functions default to overwrite = TRUE to handle non-atomic arrow::write_parquet() writes — a crashed task leaves a partial file; retry with overwrite = TRUE replaces it cleanly.
Narrow glob _markers\\.parquet$ used in R/queries.R to exclude genotype files from the markers DuckDB view (genotype files share the marker_sets/ directory but have a different schema).
Database integration workflow made the default path; legacy R assessment is now opt-in via --legacy_assess.




PR #95 — Documentation Site (2026-02-18)

Added

Quarto website in docs/ published to GitHub Pages at https://andersenlab.github.io/nemascan-sims-nf/.
One page per pipeline phase: Marker Set Generation, Trait Simulation, GWAS Mapping, QTL Detection & Assessment, Database Structure.
Development guide covering local rendering, publishing, testing, HPC execution, and project structure.




PR #93 — Cross-Validation Framework (2026-02-17)

Added

cross-validation.qmd documentation page explaining concordance methodology.
Cross-validation assessment script computing concordance scores (detection, designation, interval overlap) between the DB path and legacy R path.

title: Phase 1: Marker Set Generation
text:
The first phase of the pipeline transforms a multi-sample VCF into LD-pruned PLINK binary filesets and a numeric genotype matrix. These outputs feed into phenotype simulation (Phase 2) and GWAS mapping (Phase 3).
The processing flow is:





%%{init: {'themeVariables': {'fontSize': '17px'}}}%%
flowchart TD
    classDef proc fill:#4e9af1,stroke:#2c6fad,color:#fff
    classDef db fill:#1a9e8f,stroke:#0e6b61,color:#fff
    classDef io fill:#f5f5f5,stroke:#999,color:#333

    VCF(["vcf.gz + strainfile"]):::io
    CONTIG["LOCAL_GET_CONTIG_INFO"]:::proc
    EXTRACT["BCFTOOLS_EXTRACT_STRAINS"]:::proc
    RENAME["BCFTOOLS_RENAME_CHROMS"]:::proc
    MS["PLINK_RECODE_MS_VCF (TO_SIMS.*)"]:::proc
    CV["PLINK_RECODE_CV_VCF (CV_TO_SIMS.*)"]:::proc
    GENO["BCFTOOLS_CREATE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX"]:::proc
    EIGEN["R_FIND_GENOTYPE_MATRIX_EIGEN"]:::proc
    WMS["DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_MARKER_SET"]:::db
    WGM["DB_MIGRATION_WRITE_GENOTYPE_MATRIX"]:::db
    P2(["Phase 2: Trait Simulation"]):::io
    P3(["Phase 3: GWAS Mapping"]):::io

    VCF --> CONTIG
    VCF --> EXTRACT
    CONTIG --> RENAME
    EXTRACT --> RENAME
    RENAME --> MS & CV
    CV --> P2
    MS --> P3
    MS --> GENO & EIGEN
    GENO --> WGM
    EIGEN --> WMS
    MS --> WMS






Each process is described below with the exact commands run, key parameters, and links to external tool documentation.
